issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. A visible replacement such as box-shadow or a solid border is required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, failing to provide a visible focus indicator. The outline-offset property alone does not render a visible ring when outline-width is zero.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and other incorrect properties (width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, clip: auto) that keep the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while preserving it for assistive technologies. This fails the purpose of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline and provides no alternative focus indicator. Users in high-contrast mode lose all focus visibility, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on button base class
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 551: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }` and add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible removes the outline with no replacement (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). Keyboard users cannot see focus within the main navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, falling below the 44px recommended size and lacking the 8px padding compensation that would bring the effective target area to 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon button width
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8, making the icon button too small for reliable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement that is not suppressed for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 768: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); min-height: 32px; border-radius: var(--radius-xl); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` and add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links rely solely on colour change (from neutral-700 to brand-primary) to indicate hover state, with no underline, border-bottom, or background highlight. This makes links indistinguishable from text for colour-blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 645: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only a negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for dropdown menu links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 588: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, making the search button's focus state invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 629: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This removes the default browser focus ring without replacement, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes content visually visible instead of hiding it. This defeats the purpose of the class and exposes helper text to sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty and does not disable the transform or transition defined in the base rule. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 803: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia. Negative word-spacing can cause letters to visually merge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.92:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background, resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.92:1. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text, making it hard to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 232: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets only outline-offset: -3px while inheriting outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. No visible focus ring is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. It should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip-path or clip: rect(0,0,0,0).
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, placing it in the 24–43px range but without sufficient surrounding padding (0px < 8px) to meet the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. With padding of 6px, total height is 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. With padding of 10px, total size is 40px, below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px with no padding, failing the 44px minimum touch target requirement.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the absolute minimum of 24px and recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links have min-height: 24px with no padding defined, resulting in a touch target smaller than the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the screen-reader-only pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at line 455 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 455: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform (rotate/perspective) and transition. The prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 1390 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active state uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, significantly impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 235: word-spacing: 0;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but the function never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1805: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the panel is displayed but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". The companion _closeInstance correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetric state that confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion opens panels using animateHeight and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3055: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not set aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion closes panels but never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false". While the panel is hidden from the accessibility tree, the trigger continues to report an expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3090: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (currently only removeClass is called, the attribute is not set).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, no status message is announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no confirmation that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu emits a custom event but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or appropriate string) before or alongside the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the open() function announces via liveRegion, the close() function does not. Users are not notified when an accordion panel collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} — collapsed').replace('{title}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-play state changes when users click the play/pause button, but these changes are not announced to screen readers. Users cannot tell if the carousel is currently rotating or paused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3355 (play): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Carousel playing');`. Line 3360 (pause): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Carousel paused');`.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept/decline missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner disappears without announcing the result. Users may not know their choice was registered or what the current consent state is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4207 (_onAccept): Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookies accepted');`. Line 4217 (_onDecline): Add `liveRegion.announce('Only essential cookies enabled');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded missing collapse announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function announces "Comparison table expanded" when opening, but provides no announcement when the table is collapsed (expanded = false).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2812: Add an else clause with `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.comparisonTableCollapsed || 'Comparison table collapsed');` when expanded is false.

fpTitles: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
testimonialsCarousel play/pause missing live region announcement
cookieConsent accept/decline missing live region announcement
pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded missing collapse announcement

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1500: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function, immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and announces the state via liveRegion, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will still report the button as collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2800: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer and updates ARIA attributes, but provides no audible confirmation to screen reader users that the menu has closed. Users may be unaware the context has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2820: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the emit() call inside the close() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu panel is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the button state out of sync with the visible panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2950: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (either via Escape key, outside click, or selecting an item), the function updates CSS and ARIA attributes but does not announce the closure to screen readers, leaving users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2970: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` immediately before the emit() call inside the _closeInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4200: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses the FAQ answer panel and updates the aria-expanded attribute, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the collapsed state via the live region. Users may not realize the content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4220: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` immediately before the closing brace of the close() function (after the onClose callback invocation).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function declares a _trap variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap. Keyboard users can tab outside the open mobile menu into the underlying page content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (focus order) and 2.4.7 (focus visible).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function deactivates the focus trap (if it existed) but never explicitly returns focus to the trigger button that opened the menu. Keyboard users are left with focus at the bottom of the page or in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2820: Add `_trigger.focus();` immediately after the `_unlockScroll();` line inside the close() function.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function adds an aria-describedby reference but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field is invalid, only that there is a description. The paired `markValid` function removes the attribute, creating a state where `aria-invalid` is never applied.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function adds the open class and announces the state change but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", causing a state mismatch where the trigger remains `aria-expanded="false"` even when the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger. The `_closeInstance` function sets it to "false". This leaves the trigger in an incorrect state when the dropdown is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function expands the FAQ panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close` function sets it to "false". Assistive technology will not reflect the expanded state correctly.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions change visible content (showing/hiding the menu) but do not write to a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes unless they manually explore.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visibility changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function hides or shows the header based on scroll direction (`header:hide`/`header:show` events) but does not announce this change via a live region. Sudden disappearance of navigation landmarks can be disorienting for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view toggle buttons lack keyboard accessibility enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function attaches a click delegate to `[data-view-mode]` elements but does not ensure they have `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` if they are not native buttons. Non-native interactive elements must be made focusable and expose their role to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks keyboard accessibility enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function queries the `_trigger` element but does not enforce `role="button"` or `tabindex="0"` if the element is not a native `<button>`. If the HTML uses a `<div>` or `<span>` for the hamburger icon, it will not be focusable or operable via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() not setting aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2407: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment and before the `addClass` calls.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle dropdown visibility but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies will not be informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2552 (in _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before the emit call. Line ~2572 (in _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The companion _closeInstance correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetric state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2548: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function updates the visual active class on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which tab is active. Toggle buttons must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3718: Inside the forEach callback, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() function animates the panel open and updates state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4232: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel opens, the _open function sets internal state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element. The _close function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3387: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() not returning focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the function deactivates the focus trap but does not move focus back to the hamburger trigger button. Keyboard users are left with no focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2440: After `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`, insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to the field but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds classes and locks scroll but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1265: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1435: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the panel and removes hidden but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2935: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The open function announces "Navigation menu opened" via liveRegion, but the close function does not announce the menu closing. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The open function announces the question expansion via liveRegion, but the close function does not announce the collapse. Screen reader users are not informed when the answer collapses.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2060: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after inst.open = true; in _openInstance.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but it does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3067: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); inside the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the content appears visually but is not announced to screen readers. This is particularly problematic when the dropdown contains important navigation options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2068: Add liveRegion.announce(`Menu opened`); after emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the code removes error classes and descriptions but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers may still report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4258: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); or removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); inside the if (result.valid) block after srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-controls on the trigger button to reference the drawer element (_drawer). Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1937: Add if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')); after _drawer is assigned.

issuesFound.title: productFilter category toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons ([data-filter-value]) act as toggle buttons that switch between active and inactive states, but the code only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3167: Add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the price values update visually and are animated, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3469: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); after the forEach loop that updates plan cards.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions expand and collapse FAQ panels but do not announce the state change via the live region. Users may not know whether the panel opened or closed, especially if focus did not move into the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4945: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); inside the open function after emit.
  Line 4972: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`); inside the close function after emit.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function creates a focus trap (_trap) but never activates it. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background content, violating the modal/dialog focus containment requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1868: Add if (_trap) _trap.activate(); after _lockScroll(); and before emit.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu opens a panel but does not trap focus within it. Keyboard users can tab out of the dropdown panel, causing the menu to remain open while focus moves to other parts of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2062: Add const trap = createFocusTrap(inst.panel, { onDeactivate: () => _closeInstance(inst) }); trap.activate(); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); and store the trap reference on the instance to deactivate later.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `open` and `close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and overlay but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1380: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line ~1400: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` utility function associates an error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to identify erroneous inputs.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets the item state to open and animates the panel, but unlike the close() function which sets aria-expanded="false", it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when an accordion item expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3420: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input implements the combobox pattern but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute when the suggestion listbox opens or closes. Assistive technologies cannot convey the visibility state of the suggestions dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2750: In the _open() function, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.
  Line 2760: In the _close() function, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer container (_drawer). Users can tab out of the drawer and access content behind the overlay, making the menu unusable for keyboard and screen reader users. The code declares a _trap variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: In the open() function, after opening the drawer, add:
  `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers in the dropdownMenu module, the code sets aria-haspopup but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for triggers whose panels are initially hidden. This leaves the expanded state ambiguous for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2280: In the register() function, after setting aria-haspopup, add:
  `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it lacks an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. This is required for the combobox pattern to indicate that the suggestion list is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2800: In the init() function, after setting role="combobox", add:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function sets item.open to true and updates classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not informed when an item expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2626: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module _onToggleClick function toggles the billing period and updates CSS classes on _toggleEl but fails to update aria-pressed or aria-checked. The element is given role="switch" but its state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2147: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters function updates the text content of the count badge (_countBadge) to reflect visible product counts. This dynamic content change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users are unaware the filter results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2006: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price display updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod function updates the text content of price elements (plan.priceDisplay) when the billing period changes. This dynamic content change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users are unaware the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2097: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button click not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action via a live region. While the keyboard shortcut for this action announces "Scrolled to top", the button click does not, leaving screen reader users unaware of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1626: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator element missing aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator _animate function updates the textContent of the element (el) to count up numbers. The element does not have aria-live="polite" or role="status", so screen readers do not announce the counting animation or the final value change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3273: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to identify invalid inputs, not just the presence of an error description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1376: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markValid function removes the error description reference but fails to set aria-invalid="false" on the field element. The invalid state persists in the accessibility tree even after the user corrects the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid helper adds the error element to aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never synchronized. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown's expanded state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter results not announced

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate the active category only through CSS class toggling. They do not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons, failing to communicate the toggle state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown pair, the trigger button is given aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but the initial aria-expanded="false" is not set, leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is initially collapsed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle missing aria-pressed state

They lack aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-checked (for the switch role), so screen readers cannot convey which period is selected.

issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid helper removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers may continue to treat the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, meaning screen readers do not announce the error state of form fields to users.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button that toggles the navigation submenu does not have an aria-expanded attribute, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons do not communicate their selected/active state to assistive technology because they lack the aria-pressed attribute.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons do not indicate which option is currently selected to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1634 (Monthly button): Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}
  Line ~1643 (Annual button): Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm broken aria-describedby reference for error message
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID ('newsletter-email-error') that is never rendered because FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, resulting in a broken ARIA reference when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1809: Pass errorId to FormGroup:
  <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive carousel slides remain accessible to screen readers because they lack aria-hidden="true", causing users to hear content from slides that are not visually displayed.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in FaqItemComponent toggles the visibility of the answer panel (isOpen state) but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users are not informed whether the FAQ item is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs track an active state (activeFilter) visually via className and tabIndex, but lack aria-pressed to convey the selected state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot identify which category filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1075: <button type="button" className={[ 'filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} >

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but fails to pass aria-invalid="true" to the underlying <input> element when invalid is true. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid even when visual error styles are applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu button that controls the mobile navigation panel has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. Assistive technology cannot announce the open/closed state of the navigation panel.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons indicate active state visually via CSS but do not communicate the pressed state to assistive technology. They function as toggle buttons and require aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div with onClick missing interactive semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop <div> has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space). Keyboard-only users cannot activate the backdrop to close the modal.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2750: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler to the overlay div, or convert to a <button> element.
  ```tsx
  <div
    className="modal-overlay"
    role="button"
    tabIndex={0}
    aria-label="Close dialog"
    onClick={onClose}
    onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); }}}
  >
  ```

issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons indicate selection visually via CSS but do not communicate the selected state programmatically to screen readers, making it impossible to know which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button in the FilterTabs map.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons show selection state visually but lack aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which billing option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the Monthly button and aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the Annual button.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation landmark lacks an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions on the page when using screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Skip navigation" to the nav element in SkipLinks.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks an accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element in HeroSection.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is styled differently but not programmatically identified with aria-current, preventing screen readers from announcing it as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element in DesktopNav.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu lacks aria-current, failing to indicate the current page to assistive technology when navigating the landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element without an accessible name, preventing users from understanding which plan the card represents when navigating by article regions.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article element in PlanCard.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button toggles a panel but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen readers from announcing the panel's current expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element in FaqItemComponent.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses mobile navigation subsections does not indicate its state to assistive technology, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm button aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The button's aria-describedby references an element that only exists in success or error states, causing a broken reference during form submission and idle states that confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Change aria-describedby to conditionally reference the status element only when it exists: aria-describedby={status === 'success' || status === 'error' ? statusId : undefined}.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrect aria-modal placement
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay instead of the dialog element, potentially causing screen readers to misinterpret the modal structure and focus scope.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div and add it to the div with role="dialog" and aria-labelledby={titleId}.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel navigation buttons contain only icon characters without accessible names via aria-label, preventing screen reader users from understanding their purpose or function.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the previous IconButton and aria-label="Next testimonial" to the next IconButton in TestimonialsCarousel.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group of trust badges lacks an accessible name to describe the group's purpose to screen reader users navigating the page structure.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Trust badges and certifications" to the div containing the trust badges.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group of call-to-action buttons lacks an accessible name, making it unclear what the button group represents when encountered out of context by screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links is a landmark region but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. When multiple nav landmarks exist on a page (e.g., main nav, footer nav), each must have a unique accessible name to allow screen reader users to distinguish between them.

issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link uses the item.current prop to apply a visual "current" class but does not include the aria-current="page" attribute. Screen reader users rely on aria-current to identify the link representing the current page within a navigation set.
